---
title: "Clean Deduped Data"
date: last-modified
date-format: long
execute:
  warning: false
  echo: false
  error: false  
format:
  typst:    
    toc: true
    papersize: a4
    fig-align: center
    margin:
      left: 2cm
      right: 2cm
      top: 2cm
      bottom: 3cm
    columns: 1
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{python}
import os

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

os.chdir(os.path.expanduser("~/GoogleDrive/_1_Projects/0_thesis/analysis"))

data_path = "/home/thhaase/Documents/synosys_masterthesis"
```

```{python}
dd_track = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(data_path, "bt_track_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_reftweets_dedup.csv"))

dd_follow = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(data_path, "bt_follow_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_reftweets_dedup.csv"))

dd = pd.concat([dd_track, dd_follow], ignore_index=True)
dd = dd.sort_values(
    by=['id', 'like_count', 'retweet_count', 'reply_count'], 
    ascending=True, 
    na_position='first'
)
dd = dd.drop_duplicates(subset=['id'], keep='last')
```

# Create new IDs
## Tweet IDs
```{python}
tweet_cols = ['id', 'to_tweetid', 'retweeted_id', 'quoted_id']

ids_dd = dd[tweet_cols].stack().unique()
ids_follow = dd_follow[tweet_cols].stack().unique()
ids_track = dd_track[tweet_cols].stack().unique()

all_tweet_ids = pd.unique(np.concatenate([ids_dd, ids_follow, ids_track]))

tweet_id_map = {original_id: new_id for new_id, original_id in enumerate(all_tweet_ids, start=1)}

for col in tweet_cols:
    dd[f'x{col}'] = dd[col].map(tweet_id_map)
    dd_follow[f'x{col}'] = dd_follow[col].map(tweet_id_map)
    dd_track[f'x{col}'] = dd_track[col].map(tweet_id_map)
```

Sanitycheck:
```{python}
dfs = {'dd': dd, 'dd_follow': dd_follow, 'dd_track': dd_track}

for name, df in dfs.items():
    print(f"Checking {name}...")
    for col in tweet_cols:
        new_col = f'x{col}'

        original_not_na = df[col].notna()
        missing_mappings = df.loc[original_not_na, new_col].isna().sum()
        
        if missing_mappings > 0:
            print(f"❌ {col}: Failed! {missing_mappings} IDs resulted in NaN (ID missing from map).")
        else:
            print(f"✅ {col}: Successfully mapped. No unintended NaNs.")
```

## User IDs
Hier weitermachen... irgendein fehler hier
```{python}
user_cols = ["user_id", "to_userid", "retweeted_user_id", "quoted_user_id", "mentioned_ids"]

ids_dd = dd[user_cols].stack().unique()
ids_follow = dd_follow[user_cols].stack().unique()
ids_track = dd_track[user_cols].stack().unique()

all_user_ids = pd.unique(np.concatenate([ids_dd, ids_follow, ids_track]))

user_id_map = {original_id: new_id for new_id, original_id in enumerate(all_user_ids, start=1)}

for col in user_cols:
    dd[f'x{col}'] = dd[col].map(user_id_map)
    dd_follow[f'x{col}'] = dd_follow[col].map(user_id_map)
    dd_track[f'x{col}'] = dd_track[col].map(user_id_map)
```

Sanitycheck:
```{python}
dfs = {'dd': dd, 'dd_follow': dd_follow, 'dd_track': dd_track}

for name, df in dfs.items():
    print(f"Checking {name}...")
    for col in user_cols:
        new_col = f'x{col}'

        original_not_na = df[col].notna()
        missing_mappings = df.loc[original_not_na, new_col].isna().sum()
        
        if missing_mappings > 0:
            print(f"❌ {col}: Failed! {missing_mappings} IDs resulted in NaN (ID missing from map).")
        else:
            print(f"✅ {col}: Successfully mapped. No unintended NaNs.")
```

```{python}
del df, original_not_na, all_tweet_ids, all_user_ids, col, dfs, ids_dd, ids_follow, ids_track, missing_mappings, name, new_col, tweet_cols, tweet_id_map, user_cols, user_id_map
```

# Match Parties

```{python}
p = pd.read_parquet(os.path.join(data_path, "politicians.parquet.gzip"))
#p = p[p['election_period_20'] == True]

p['user_screen_name'] = p['x_url'].str.replace(r"https://x.com/|/$", "", regex=True)

dd = dd.merge(p, on='user_screen_name', how='left')
dd_follow = dd_follow.merge(p, on='user_screen_name', how='left')
dd_track = dd_track.merge(p, on='user_screen_name', how='left')
```

```{python}
# --- RECEIVER MATCH (New Addition) ---
# This ensures that if a tweet replies to an MP, we know the target is an MP.

# A. Create a mapping of [User ID -> Party] for politicians
# We first find all rows in our data where we successfully identified a politician
# and grab their User ID.
known_politicians = pd.concat([
    dd[['xuser_id', 'party']].dropna(),
    dd_follow[['xuser_id', 'party']].dropna(),
    dd_track[['xuser_id', 'party']].dropna()
]).drop_duplicates(subset='xuser_id')

# Rename for merging onto the 'to_userid' column
known_politicians = known_politicians.rename(columns={
    'xuser_id': 'xto_userid', 
    'party': 'target_party'
})

# B. Merge this 'target_party' onto our main dataframes
# Now, if xto_userid matches a known politician, we get their party in 'target_party'
dd = dd.merge(known_politicians, on='xto_userid', how='left')
dd_follow = dd_follow.merge(known_politicians, on='xto_userid', how='left')
dd_track = dd_track.merge(known_politicians, on='xto_userid', how='left')
```

# Save Datasets
```{python}
dd.to_parquet(f'{data_path}/dd.parquet')
dd_follow.to_parquet(f'{data_path}/dd_follow.parquet')
dd_track.to_parquet(f'{data_path}/dd_track.parquet')
```


```{python}
%reset -f

```
